chief_complaint: A woman in her 50s presents with dehydration, fatigue, respiratory distress, and severe metabolic acidosis.
history: The patient has a 15-year history of type 2 diabetes and chronic heart failure. She had been taking metformin and sodium-glucose cotransporter 2 (SGLT2) inhibitors. She recently experienced dehydration and fluid restriction due to working in hot conditions.
exam: The patient showed signs of dehydration, fatigue, respiratory distress, hypotension, tachypnea, and hypothermia.
labs: Severe metabolic acidosis, lactic acidosis, acute renal failure, hypoglycemia, and elevated beta-hydroxybutyrate level.
correct_diagnosis: Concurrent Metformin-Associated Lactic Acidosis and Euglycemic Diabetic Ketoacidosis
differentials: Concurrent Metformin-Associated Lactic Acidosis and Euglycemic Diabetic Ketoacidosis
correct_ranking: Concurrent Metformin-Associated Lactic Acidosis and Euglycemic Diabetic Ketoacidosis
